note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/NumberField/InfinitePlace/TotallyRealComplex.lean:47 — class IsTotallyReal (K) [Field K] : all infinite places of K are real; docstring states exactly this definition.
Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Connectivity/EdgeConnectivity.lean:35 — def IsEdgeConnected (G k) := ∀ u v, G.IsEdgeReachable k u v, i.e. G is k-edge-connected.

Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Pseudo/Defs.lean:432 — def sphere x ε := {y | dist y x = ε}, generic in any (pseudo)metric space; the n-sphere is the specific case Metric.sphere (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (n+1))) 1, given manifold structure in Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Instances/Sphere.lean.

Mathlib/NumberTheory/ADEInequality.lean:231 — theorem classification (p q r : ℕ+) : 1 < sumInv {p,q,r} ↔ Admissible {p,q,r}, classifying solutions into A'/D'/E6/E7/E8 families; file docstring ties this directly to 'the classification of Dynkin diagrams, root systems, and semisimple Lie algebras' but this is the (p,q,r)-inequality classification, not a statement of the ADE Dynkin-diagram/singularity classification itself.

Mathlib/Data/List/PeriodicityLemma.lean:191 — theorem HasPeriod.gcd, the Periodicity Lemma / Fine-and-Wilf theorem for words (Lists): a word with periods p and q of length ≥ p+q-gcd(p,q) has period gcd(p,q). One classical result within the field, not a formalization of the field itself.
